beats.narration: A can of penetrant, a pail of emulsifier, a can of developer. They look simple, but the rules around them protect the sensitivity you think you have.

payload.title: MATERIALS & PRECAUTIONS
payload.sub: What's in the cans, and how to keep it honest.

beats.narration: Here's the rule that ties it together. Penetrant, emulsifier, remover, and developer are qualified as one family. Mix brands across stations, and the qualification is void.

payload.heading: Qualified together, used together
payload.note: ONE QUALIFIED FAMILY — CROSS-BRAND SWAP VOIDS IT

beats.narration: Contamination is the silent killer. Water ruins washability, penetrant in dry developer glows as false specks, and stray residues kill fluorescence outright.
Then the precautions. Standard materials hold to a temperature band. Some alloys need low-sulfur, low-halogen formulas. And aerosols, vapors, and oils demand respect.
So the discipline is simple. Keep the family together, keep it clean, stay in the band. Protect the materials, the part, and yourself.

payload.sub: Protect the materials, the part, and yourself.
